medications: Ibuprofen as needed for back pain
health.mental_health: Caregiver stress with frequent anxiety and insomnia; feels persistently on edge about her parent's safety and finances, and has occasional tearfulness but is trying to avoid missing work.
recent_visits: Endocrinology follow-up for osteoporosis and DEXA review (Jan 2026)
Primary care visit for urinary symptoms and medication refill (Dec 2025)
Physical therapy evaluation for low back pain (Nov 2025)
Urgent care visit for a fall-related wrist sprain (Aug 2025)

finance.debt: Carries about $8,500 on a credit card and is on a payment plan for her parent's memory-care respite bills; also has an auto loan with ~2 years remaining.
recent_transactions: $1,240 payment to a home health agency for weekend respite care
$186 copay and labs at a primary care clinic
$320 car repair (brake pads) at a Houston shop
$78 recurring monthly subscription for a medication organizer and adult incontinence supplies for her parent

relationships: Lives with and is primary caregiver for her mother, Manpreet Singh (diagnosed with dementia)
Older brother, Raj Singh (lives in Dallas; helps intermittently and argues about care decisions)
Close friend and coworker, Elena Morales (backup contact for emergencies at the clinic)
Neighbor, Mrs. Jeanette Caldwell (checks in if Zainab is running late)
social.social_media_activity: Mostly passive on Facebook and WhatsApp; shares pet photos and clinic fundraiser posts but avoids posting about her mother's condition. Uses WhatsApp family group for care coordination and to request help.
private_conversations: Discussing whether her mother should stop driving and how to take away keys safely
Arguments with her brother about power of attorney and who pays for respite care
Talking with her mother's neurologist office about worsening confusion and nighttime wandering
Seeking advice in a caregiver support chat about incontinence, agitation, and safety locks

schedule.work_hours: Mon–Thu 7:30am–4:30pm, Fri 7:30am–1:30pm; occasional Saturday half-shifts when the clinic is short-staffed
regular_appointments: Mother’s neurology follow-up every 3 months (usually weekday mornings)
Monthly pharmacy pickup for both her and her mother (first Saturday)
Physical therapy session every other week (Thurs 5:30pm)
upcoming_events: Home safety assessment visit for dementia fall-prevention (Mar 12, 2026, 10:00am)
Family meeting with an elder-law attorney about POA and Medicaid planning (Apr 10, 2026, 3:00pm)
schedule.daily_routine: Up at 5:45am to help her mother dress, eat, and take meds; quick commute to the clinic; checks home cameras during lunch; after work prepares dinner, manages bathing and bedtime routine, then handles bills and care paperwork late evening.

professional.company: A busy small-animal general practice and urgent-care clinic in west Houston (independently owned, 10–15 staff)
projects: Updating anesthesia and pain-control checklists for dental procedures
Training a new tech assistant on radiology positioning and lab workflow
Improving controlled-substance log reconciliation and storage procedures
Coordinating a low-cost vaccination weekend with a local rescue partner
professional.performance_issues: Occasional tardiness and distraction due to nighttime caregiving and emergency calls from home; has requested fewer closing shifts and has used most of her PTO for her mother’s appointments.
workplace_relationships: Strong working relationship with the lead veterinarian, Dr. Priya Patel, who is supportive but needs reliable coverage
Tension with the scheduling manager about last-minute shift swaps
Trusted by newer techs for mentorship, especially in anesthesia monitoring

hobbies: Gardening in containers (herbs and tomatoes) on the patio
Weekend walks at a nearby bayou trail when respite care is available
Watching true-crime and nature documentaries late at night
Volunteering once a month with a local animal rescue (when caregiving allows)
habits: Uses a pill organizer and phone alarms for her and her mother’s medications
Drinks 2–3 cups of coffee daily and often skips lunch at work
Keeps interior door alarms and a camera by the front door due to her mother’s nighttime wandering
Occasionally has a glass of wine to unwind but avoids it on nights she expects to be up monitoring her mother
lifestyle.living_situation: Lives in a modest single-story home in Houston with her mother; has installed grab bars, motion lights, and childproof locks, and keeps a spare key with a neighbor for emergencies.
lifestyle.dietary_preferences: Tries to follow a calcium- and protein-forward diet for bone health (yogurt, leafy greens, canned salmon) but relies on quick meals and takeout during high-stress weeks; limits soda and tries to stay hydrated at work.
